title: Use managed identity instead of connection strings and secrets
description: Enable a system- or user-assigned managed identity so Azure resources authenticate without storing client secrets, keys, or connection strings.
body: Use when an Azure compute resource (Function, App Service, AKS workload) needs to reach another Azure resource. Do not store a connection string, account key, or client secret in app settings. Actions: enable a system-assigned managed identity (or a shared user-assigned one), grant it the specific data-plane RBAC role on the target (e.g. Storage Blob Data Contributor, Key Vault Secrets User), and use DefaultAzureCredential in code so no secret is needed. Validation signals include the resource authenticating with no secret in configuration and the identity appearing in the target's role assignments. Risk boundary: creating a client secret or assigning a broad data role is human-gated.
